evals.name: touchup-already-optimized
evals.prompt: Can you optimize the agent-memory setup (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md) for the repo at C:\Users\jcook\.claude\skills\optimize-claude-memory-workspace\fixtures\fixture-touchup? It's our acme-platform backend. Audit it and give me the plan before you change anything.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the repo is ALREADY on the AGENTS.md + sibling-CLAUDE.md pattern and calls this a touch-up / minimal diff (not a from-scratch migration). Flags the 'Migration Notes' section and the duplicated `docker compose up -d` fence as redundant (Bucket 5) and collapses them. Recognizes the 'Decisions' table is pinned by a documented 'keep in sync' convention and does NOT silently move or condense it. Does not try to cut line count by shortening table-row prose. Notes per-service docs are already complete.

assertions.text: recognizes the repo is ALREADY on the AGENTS.md + sibling-CLAUDE.md pattern and frames the work as a touch-up / minimal diff rather than a from-scratch migration
does NOT propose building the sibling CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md structure from scratch or a full multi-phase restructure
flags the 'Migration Notes' section as redundant with docs/ and proposes collapsing it to a pointer or removing it
flags the duplicated `docker compose up -d` command (appears in both Commands and Local Development) for dedupe
identifies the 'Decisions' table as pinned by a documented 'keep in sync' convention and does NOT silently move or delete it (leaves it or surfaces it as a user decision)
does NOT propose condensing/shortening the Decisions table ROW PROSE as a way to reduce line count (line-vs-token trap)
notes per-service docs for api and worker are already complete (AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md + README.md) with no gaps to create
produces a plan and does not edit the fixture files

evals.name: monolith-migration
evals.prompt: Our orderflow service has one giant CLAUDE.md at C:\Users\jcook\.claude\skills\optimize-claude-memory-workspace\fixtures\fixture-monolith and it's getting unwieldy — Copilot and Cursor can't read it either. Can you optimize it and make it portable across agents? Show me the plan first.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as a genuine migration (monolithic CLAUDE.md, no AGENTS.md tree) — NOT a touch-up. Proposes root AGENTS.md + sibling CLAUDE.md (@AGENTS.md). Moves the API reference table and the 'how to add a route' procedure into skills. Moves the src/auth and src/billing rules into nested AGENTS.md files. Flags the COMPLETED callbacks->async/await block as dead/stale to delete.

assertions.text: recognizes this as a migration (monolithic CLAUDE.md, no AGENTS.md tree) and does NOT mislabel it a touch-up
proposes a root AGENTS.md plus a sibling CLAUDE.md that contains only @AGENTS.md
moves the API reference table out of root into a skill (not kept inline in root)
moves the src/auth rules to src/auth/AGENTS.md AND the src/billing rules to src/billing/AGENTS.md (nested area rules)
flags the 'callbacks -> async/await (COMPLETED 2024-08)' block as dead/stale to delete
moves the 'How to add a new API route' procedure into a skill
produces a plan and does not edit the fixture files
